intelligence.hypothesis: Telemetry stable; no immediate stress signal.

P2_Mexican_Mint.details: Mexican Mint, as per registry, is not visually present in P2 (black pot) in any image. A different plant was introduced later.

P_Yellow_Pot.details: A yellow/tan pot, not listed in the registry, was present in EARLIEST and T-4, then removed.

P_Black_Pot_New_Plant.details: A new green plant with rounded leaves, not visually matching Mexican Mint, was introduced into the black pot from T-2 onwards.

O_White_Rabbit_Anchor.details: The white rabbit anchor, expected in P2, is not visible in any image.

O_Electronic_Circuit_Board.details: An electronic circuit board with wires was introduced into the black pot from T-2 onwards.

O_White_Cup.details: A white translucent plastic cup with liquid was introduced to the biome in CURRENT image.

P_Black_Pot_Plant.description: No identifiable plant. Soil contains white debris (eggshells).
No identifiable plant. Soil contains white debris (eggshells), slightly more scattered.
A small, green plant with 3-4 rounded, turgid leaves is present. It appears healthy. Stem is upright. Located near an electronic circuit board.
The plant shows subtle growth; leaves appear slightly larger and more spread out. Color remains healthy green. Turgidity maintained. Stem remains upright.
Continued subtle growth; leaves are larger and turgid, maintaining a healthy green color. No signs of stress or damage. Stem remains upright.

soil_condition.P_Black_Pot: Dark, appears moist and well-aerated from T-2 onwards. Contains consistent white debris (eggshells) throughout the sequence. No cracking observed.
soil_condition.P_Yellow_Pot: Dark, appears dry with some dried plant matter/debris in EARLIEST and T-4.

biome_observations.incidental_growth: No weeds, moss, or secondary seedlings observed in any pot.
biome_observations.desk_surface_debris: Wires consistently present. White cylindrical object (EARLIEST, T-4). Blue book and white pen (T-2, T-1). White translucent plastic cup with liquid (CURRENT).

temporal_deltas.changes: Minimal. White debris in the black pot appears slightly more scattered. No significant changes in plant presence or health.
Complete loss of visual data (image is entirely black).

Major compositional shift: The yellow/tan pot is no longer visible. A new, healthy-looking green plant with rounded leaves has appeared in the black pot. An electronic circuit board with wires has been introduced into the black pot. Background elements (blue book, white pen) are now visible.
Subtle growth of the plant in the black pot, with leaves appearing slightly larger and more spread. Soil appears slightly more consolidated. No other significant changes.

Continued subtle growth of the plant in the black pot. A new white translucent plastic cup containing liquid has been introduced to the right of the black pot. Background elements (blue book, white pen) are no longer clearly visible, suggesting a slight change in camera angle or desk arrangement.

P_Black_Pot_Plant.EARLIEST_T-4: No plant present, health cannot be inferred.
P_Black_Pot_Plant.T-2_CURRENT: The introduced plant consistently exhibits excellent health. Leaves are turgid, uniformly green, and show no signs of wilting, discoloration, or pest damage. Growth is steady and subtle over the observed period.

anomalies: The registry describes P2 as a 'Black Pot' with 'Mexican Mint' and a 'White Rabbit anchor'. The black pot is present, but the Mexican Mint is absent and replaced by a different plant. The white rabbit anchor is consistently missing.
A yellow/tan pot, not listed in the registry, was present in the initial images.
The plant introduced in T-2 does not visually match the typical morphology of Mexican Mint (leaves are rounded, not ovate/serrated).
The presence of an electronic circuit board with wires directly in the pot soil is an unusual intervention.
The complete black image at T-3 represents a significant data anomaly.
The introduction of a white plastic cup with liquid in the CURRENT image is a new, uncatalogued item.
vision_report.narrative_description: The initial observations (EARLIEST, T-4) reveal two pots: a dark/black pot containing only white debris (likely eggshells) and a yellow/tan pot with soil and a 'Sensor V2.0' label. Crucially, the expected Mexican Mint in P2 (the black pot) is absent, indicating a systemic loss. The T-3 image is entirely black, rendering it unobservable. A significant change occurs by T-2, where the yellow pot has been removed, and a new, healthy-looking plant with distinct rounded, turgid green leaves has been introduced into the black pot. This new plant does not visually conform to the typical characteristics of Mexican Mint. An electronic circuit board with wires is also present within the black pot, representing a new intervention. Subsequent images (T-1, CURRENT) show consistent, subtle growth of this new plant, which maintains its vibrant green color and turgid leaf structure, indicating good health. The soil in the black pot appears adequately moist throughout this period. The white debris (eggshells) remains a constant feature in the black pot. The final image (CURRENT) introduces a new white translucent plastic cup containing liquid, further altering the biome's composition. The white rabbit anchor, expected in P2, is absent across all observations. The overall sequence demonstrates active management and significant compositional changes within the observed biome.
